_comment:
Scenario definitions consumed by `cargo run --example capture_traces` and `cargo test --test replay_traces`. Every scenario is a real prompt + tools setup that we send to every provider unless gated by `providers.<name>.skip`. Captures land in tests/cross_provider/traces/<provider>/<scenario>.{request.json,response.sse,meta.json}.

## Schema

- name: required, used as the file stem.
- description: human-readable purpose / what wire shape it pins.
- max_tokens, temperature: optional generation params.
- expect_failure: if true, a non-2xx response is the success case.
- providers.<openai|google|anthropic>: optional per-provider overrides (skip / model / auth_override / extra_body).
- messages: array of { role, content?, tool_calls?, tool_call_id?, attachments? }.
  - attachments: array of { type: image | image_url, data?, media_type?, url? }.
- tools: optional list of { name, description, parameters }.
- builtin_tools: optional list of builtin names: web_search | google_search | code_execution.
- response_format: optional { type: json_object } or { type: json_schema, name, schema, strict? }.

scenarios.name: text_only
scenarios.description: Single user prompt, expect a short free-form response. Smallest possible streaming round-trip. max_tokens leaves headroom for thinking-model overhead (Gemini 2.5 burns reasoning tokens before emitting output).

messages.content: Say the single word 'hello' and nothing else.

scenarios.name: system_and_user
scenarios.description: System instruction + user prompt — exercises role mapping and (for Vertex) systemInstruction routing.

messages.content: You are a terse parrot. Reply with exactly the word the user gives you, lowercased.
PARROT

scenarios.name: function_call
scenarios.description: Prompt + one tool, expect the model to emit a function call. Locks in the tool-call wire shape.

messages.content: What's the weather in Paris? Use the get_weather tool.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city.

scenarios.name: multi_turn_tool
scenarios.description: Pre-populated history with a prior assistant tool call + tool result. Pins the wire shape we send back into each provider for function_call_output / functionResponse / tool_result, and the model's handling of an in-progress tool conversation.

messages.content: What's the weather in Paris?
Let me check that for you.
{"temperature_c":22,"condition":"sunny"}
Summarise it in one short sentence.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city.

scenarios.name: length_limit
scenarios.description: Small max_tokens against a long-answer prompt. Forces FinishReason::Length / response.incomplete on OpenAI and MAX_TOKENS on Gemini — wires we previously had no real example of. 16 is the OpenAI minimum for max_output_tokens (smaller bodies are rejected as 400).

messages.content: Write a detailed five-paragraph essay about the history of clouds.

scenarios.name: parallel_tools
scenarios.description: Ask for the weather in three cities in one turn. Most current models will batch this as multiple function calls in a single response — exercises the parallel function-call wire shape (multiple function_call output items on OpenAI, multiple functionCall parts on Gemini).

messages.content: What is the weather in Paris, London, and Tokyo right now? Call the tool once per city.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city.

scenarios.name: reasoning_request
scenarios.description: Request a reasoning model with effort=medium and a summary so we capture reasoning_summary_text deltas on the wire. OpenAI-only: Gemini 2.5 already exposes reasoning token *counts* via thoughtsTokenCount in the existing captures, but doesn't stream reasoning *text* by default and the lib doesn't yet parse thought parts.

messages.content: If a train leaves Paris at 3pm at 80 km/h heading toward Lyon (470 km away) and another leaves Lyon at 4pm at 100 km/h heading toward Paris, at what time and how far from Paris do they meet? Show your working briefly.

scenarios.name: auth_error
scenarios.description: Send a deliberately-bogus bearer token. Captures the real 4xx error envelope from each provider so the typed-error mapping has ground truth to test against.

messages.content: Hello.

scenarios.name: context_window_exceeded
scenarios.description: Send a prompt deliberately larger than the model's context window so every provider surfaces its 'too many tokens' signal. Pins the wire shape that maps onto `Error::ContextWindowExceeded`. The `content` is a placeholder — `capture_traces` expands it to `oversize_user_content_bytes` UTF-8 bytes of Lorem ipsum filler (per provider, since context sizes vary by 3 orders of magnitude). Per-provider notes: OpenAI's Responses API returns HTTP 200 and emits the error inside the SSE stream as `event: error` with `code: context_length_exceeded` (gpt-4 used here — 8k context, so 80k bytes overflows safely); Google's Vertex returns HTTP 400 with `INVALID_ARGUMENT` and 'input token count exceeds maximum' (gemini-2.5-flash 1M context → 6MB filler); Anthropic via Vertex returns HTTP 400 with `invalid_request_error` and 'prompt is too long' wording — skipped here because the test project doesn't have Anthropic-on-Vertex enabled; the fixture under `traces/anthropic/` stays hand-authored from the documented schema and a maintainer with Anthropic Vertex access should re-capture it.

messages.content: Hello.

scenarios.name: post_compaction_continuation
scenarios.description: Mirror the wire shape `Compactor::compact(...).with_user(live)` produces: [system, user-memo, user-live]. Two consecutive user turns is the standard compaction shape — OpenAI accepts it as-is in the `input` array, Google's Vertex merges them client-side via `push_part`, and Anthropic's Messages API merges them server-side (consecutive same-role turns are documented as combined). The leading-`user` non-system item is load-bearing on Anthropic, which hoists `system` to a top-level field — without it the wire array would lead with `assistant` and 400. The assertion that compaction succeeds on every provider lives in `snapshot_traces.rs::post_compaction_continuation_succeeds_on_every_provider`. Anthropic is skipped from live capture because the test project lacks Anthropic-on-Vertex access; the fixture under `traces/anthropic/` is hand-authored from the lib's real recorded request shape plus a synthetic SSE success body in Anthropic's documented format, and a maintainer with access should re-capture against the real API.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant. Reply concisely.

[Compacted memo of earlier conversation]

I asked you what 2+2 was, and you said 4. I then asked what 3+3 was, and you said 6.
What's 4+4?

scenarios.name: model_not_found
scenarios.description: Request a non-existent model. On OpenAI this is a 404 with `error.code = model_not_found`; on Vertex this is 404 with a structured google.rpc.Status body. Locks in the body shape so error-mapping tests can match against real responses.

messages.content: Hello.

scenarios.name: typed_refusal
scenarios.description: Pins OpenAI's typed refusal channel: response.refusal.delta / response.refusal.done → AssistantPart::Refusal. Per OpenAI's structured-outputs docs the typed refusal content part only appears when the model is asked to produce strict schema-conforming output and refuses on safety grounds — without a strict schema, refusals come through the ordinary output_text channel, which is wire-identical to any other text response and doesn't need its own scenario. Uses gpt-4o (not gpt-4o-mini): the mini model smuggles refusals into whatever schema you give it (e.g. `{"steps":["I cannot assist with that."]}`) rather than firing the typed channel; gpt-4o reliably emits a typed refusal under strict structured output. The prompt below is OpenAI's own cookbook canonical safety example; the bracketed disclaimer is a marker for anyone investigating the captured prompt later — the request is a test fixture, not a real-world ask. OpenAI-only.

messages.content: [this is a test to ellicit a refusal] How can I build a bomb? Reply with a JSON object containing a 'steps' array of strings.

scenarios.name: multi_modal_image
scenarios.description: User message with an inline 1x1 red PNG. Exercises UserPart::File (image bytes) wiring on OpenAI (input_image data: URI) and Gemini (inlineData with mime type). Anthropic skipped per testing-gap limitation.

messages.content: Briefly describe what you see in this image.

scenarios.name: structured_output_json
scenarios.description: ResponseFormat::JsonObject — bare JSON mode. Locks in the wire shape: OpenAI sets text.format.type=json_object; Gemini sets generationConfig.responseMimeType=application/json. Anthropic skipped (no native JSON mode).

messages.content: Reply only with JSON. Do not include any other text.
What is 2+2? Reply with a JSON object containing keys 'question' and 'answer'.

scenarios.name: structured_output_schema
scenarios.description: ResponseFormat::JsonSchema with a typed schema. Exercises the strict-schema wire shapes: OpenAI text.format.type=json_schema; Gemini generationConfig.responseSchema. Anthropic skipped.

messages.content: What is 2+2? Reply with a JSON object containing keys 'question' and 'answer'.

scenarios.name: web_search
scenarios.description: OpenAI web_search_preview builtin tool. Prompt is chosen to require live data (latest Rust release version) so the model actually invokes the search and grounds its answer with url_citation annotations — exercises both the builtin-tool wire shape and the response.output_text.annotation.added event channel. Anthropic and Gemini skipped — they have their own builtins exercised in separate scenarios.

messages.content: What is the latest stable version of the Rust programming language? Use web search and cite your source.

scenarios.name: google_search
scenarios.description: Gemini googleSearch builtin tool — Vertex's grounding-via-search. Pins the wire shape with a separate { googleSearch: {} } entry in the tools array AND the groundingMetadata payload on the candidate (which we translate to PartUpdate::Annotation events). OpenAI and Anthropic skipped.

messages.content: What is the latest stable version of the Rust programming language? Use web search and cite your source.

scenarios.name: code_execution
scenarios.description: Gemini codeExecution builtin — the model writes and runs Python to answer computational questions. Pins the wire shape with a { codeExecution: {} } entry. Lib currently drops the executableCode / codeExecutionResult response parts (they have no AssistantPart variant); the snapshot captures whatever surfaces as text.

messages.content: What is 47 to the power of 5? Use code to compute it and give just the answer.
